cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: The legal right to vote in political elections

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: The amendment prohibiting the denial of voting rights based on race, color, or previous condition of servitude

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: The amendment granting women the right to vote, ratified in 1920

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: The amendment abolishing poll taxes in federal elections, ratified in 1964

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: The amendment lowering the voting age from 21 to 18, ratified in 1971

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: The percentage of eligible voters who actually cast ballots in an election

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: A voting model in which citizens weigh the costs and benefits of voting and vote when they believe their vote will make a difference

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: Voting based on evaluation of an incumbent's past performance rather than future promises

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: Voting based on a candidate's promises about future policies and plans

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: Voting for candidates of the same party across all races on a ballot

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: Voting for candidates of different parties on the same ballot

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: A voter who does not consistently support one political party and may switch between parties from election to election

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: Elections held between presidential elections to choose members of Congress and state officials, which typically have lower voter turnout

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: An election in which voters select candidates to represent each party in the general election

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: A primary election in which only registered party members may vote for that party's candidates

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: A primary election in which any registered voter may participate, regardless of party affiliation

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: A meeting of party members at the local level to select delegates and discuss platform issues, used in some states instead of primaries

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: The system in which each state's group of electors formally selects the president, with the winner needing 270 out of 538 votes

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: An electoral system in which the candidate who receives the most votes in a state wins all of that state's electoral votes

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: Competitive states where neither major party has a clear advantage and that receive heavy campaign attention

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: The electoral advantage that current officeholders have over challengers, including name recognition, staff, and franking privileges

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: The privilege allowing members of Congress to send official mail to constituents at no personal cost

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: Assistance provided by a member of Congress to a constituent with a specific problem involving a government agency

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: An organization that collects and donates money to political candidates on behalf of a specific interest group

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: An independent-expenditure-only committee that can raise and spend unlimited funds to support or oppose candidates but cannot donate directly to campaigns or coordinate with them

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: A tax-exempt political organization that can raise unlimited soft money for issue advocacy but cannot directly support or oppose specific candidates

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: A tax-exempt social welfare organization that can engage in political activity but is not required to disclose its donors

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: The practice of attempting to influence government officials and legislation on behalf of an interest group

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: A lobbying strategy that mobilizes ordinary citizens to contact their representatives and pressure them on specific issues

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: The movement of individuals between positions in government and positions in the private sector, particularly lobbying firms

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: The problem in which individuals benefit from a group's efforts without contributing, reducing incentive for collective action

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: Organizations that connect citizens to government, including political parties, interest groups, elections, and the media

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: A citizen's psychological attachment to a particular political party that guides voting behavior

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: A minor political party that competes with the two major parties, often focusing on specific issues or ideologies

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: An electoral system in which seats are distributed among parties in proportion to the percentage of votes they receive

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: An electoral system in which only one representative is elected per geographic district, favoring a two-party system

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: Campaign contributions that are regulated by federal law and given directly to candidates with set limits

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: Previously unregulated campaign contributions given to political parties for party-building activities, now restricted by the BCRA

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: The 2002 Bipartisan Campaign Reform Act that banned soft money contributions to national parties and restricted issue ads near elections

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: The independent federal agency responsible for enforcing campaign finance laws and regulating political contributions

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: Citizens United v. Federal Election Commission (2010)
cards.answer: Ruled that political spending by corporations and unions is protected free speech; struck down limits on independent expenditures, leading to the creation of Super PACs

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: Baker v. Carr (1962)
cards.answer: Established that redistricting disputes are justiciable and courts can rule on them, leading to the one person, one vote principle

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: Media coverage that focuses on which candidate is winning or losing rather than on substantive policy issues

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: The media's power to influence which issues the public considers important by choosing which stories to cover

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: The way media presents a story through selective emphasis, shaping how the audience interprets the issue

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: The media's influence on the standards people use to evaluate political leaders by emphasizing certain issues before a public evaluation

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: A brief, memorable excerpt from a speech or statement used in news coverage, often reducing complex positions to a few seconds

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: Digital platforms and online communication tools that have changed how political information is shared and consumed

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: A factor that increases voter turnout in elections

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: A factor that decreases voter turnout

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: An example of a third party that affected the outcome of a presidential election
cards.answer: Ross Perot's Reform Party in 1992 drew votes that may have cost George H.W. Bush the election

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: An example of how interest groups influence policy beyond direct lobbying
cards.answer: Filing amicus curiae briefs in Supreme Court cases

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: The requirement in most states that citizens must register to vote before Election Day, which can reduce turnout

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: A coalition of groups that align with a political party, such as labor unions with Democrats or evangelical Christians with Republicans

cards.subject: ap-government
cards.prompt: An impact that Citizens United v. FEC had on American elections
cards.answer: Allowed unlimited independent expenditures by corporations and unions, leading to the rise of Super PACs
alternate_answers: Increased the role of money in politics
